title: Hypertext Navigation: The AI as a Connector
content:
Hypertext Navigation: The AI as a Connector
This remains the core mechanic, but with a twist. When you click on a word in one of your notes:
If a note for that word exists: You are taken directly to your existing note, just like a standard wiki link.
If no note exists: This is where the magic happens. The AI doesn't just give you a generic definition. It performs several actions:
Semantic Search: It searches your entire knowledge base for passages, sentences, or other notes that are conceptually related to the clicked word.
Dynamic Synthesis: It generates a new, temporary "synthesis page" that summarizes how this new concept relates to your existing knowledge. It might say, "You haven't written about 'Quantum Entanglement' directly, but it relates to your notes on 'Philosophy of Time' and your project brief for 'Project Stardust'. Here are the relevant excerpts..."
Prompted Creation: This synthesis page would end with a prompt: "Would you like to create a permanent note for 'Quantum Entanglement'?" This turns passive exploration into an active process of knowledge creation.
The "Random" Button Becomes "Resurface"
context: From document: This application, "Infinite Wiki," is a dynamic, AI-powered hypertext… — chunk 5 (chunk 5/11)
